records.ety: [F. litire, LL. lectaria, fr. L. lectus couch, bed. See Lie to be prostrated, and cf. Coverlet.]

defs: A bed or stretcher so arranged that a person, esp. a sick or wounded person, may be easily carried in or upon it.
quotes: There is a litter ready; lay him in 't.

defs: Straw, hay, etc., scattered on a floor, as bedding for animals to rest on; also, a covering of straw for plants.
quotes: To crouch in litter of your stable planks.
Take off the litter from your kernel beds.

defs: Things lying scattered about in a manner indicating slovenliness; scattered rubbish.
quotes: Strephon, who found the room was void. Stole in, and took a strict survey Of all the litter as it lay.

defs: Disorder or untidiness resulting from scattered rubbish, or from thongs lying about uncared for; as, a room in a state of litter.
The young brought forth at one time, by a cat, dog, sow or other multiparous animal, taken collectively. Also Fig.
quotes: A wolf came to a sow, and very kindly offered to take care of her litter.
Reflect upon that numerous litter of strange, senseless opinions that crawl about the world.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Littered (lt"trd); p. pr. & vb. n. Littering.]

defs: To supply with litter, as cattle; to cover with litter, as the floor of a stall.
quotes: Tell them how they litter their jades.
For his ease, well littered was the floor.

defs: To put into a confused or disordered condition; to strew with scattered articles; as, to litter a room.
quotes: The room with volumes littered round.

defs: To give birth to; to bear; -- said of brutes, esp. those which produce more than one at a birth, and also of human beings, in abhorrence or contempt.
quotes: We might conceive that dogs were created blind, because we observe they were littered so with us.
The son that she did litter here, A freckled whelp hagborn.

defs: To be supplied with litter as bedding; to sleep or make one's bed in litter.
quotes: The inn Where he and his horse littered.
A desert . . . where the she-wolf still littered.
